name: Q1 2026 Dinner
description: Wide-ranging discussion on the future of software engineering, the identity crisis between craftspeople and builders, rapid prototyping, AI economics, multi-platform product strategy, and financial data intelligence.

topics.text: Should junior developers learn traditional coding? The hard part was never writing code — it's requirements gathering and navigating ambiguity
Identity crisis — craftsperson vs builder; AI threatens those who love coding for its own sake but empowers outcome-focused builders
Rapid prototyping shifting consulting — two-week delivery cycles replacing months-long scoping; networking remains primary path to work
AI economics and the SaaS paradox — unit economics get worse at scale with inference costs; subsidised era will end
Multi-platform product strategy — Reps AI sports coach deployed across web, iOS, Claude Code skill, and MCP; interface is commoditised
AI in education — Pearson's AI-first platform, university RAG project, generational split in AI adoption
AI tool adoption in teams — Claude Code adoption experiences; hands-on training beats documentation
Image generation and creative AI — product photography using AI replacing expensive studio shoots
Financial data and market intelligence — making proprietary data conversational through LLMs; generative UI for dashboards
